checks.release_boundary: This probe proves direct server Chat tool behavior for MiMo JANG_2L. It does not prove the Electron app, media, installed app, or release readiness.

prompt: Use the run_command tool exactly once to create a file named real_ui_tool_probe_1.txt in the configured working directory. Write the text REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_ONE into that file. After the tool result is returned, reply briefly in English and include REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_ONE once.

auto_nonstream.raw_preview: {"error":{"message":"Prompt too long (tokenized prompt: ~4,754 tokens). This would need ~1.9GB of KV cache memory, exceeding the configured prompt/context limit of ~4,096 tokens. Shorten the prompt, raise --max-prompt-tokens, or use a model/session with a larger prompt/context cap.","type":"invalid_request_error","code":"prompt_too_long"}}

tool_surface.system_prompt:
You are an expert software engineer with direct access to the project's file system, terminal, and the web.

You have tools available for file I/O (read, write, edit, patch, copy, move, delete, create directories, read images), search and navigation (list directories, project tree, search file contents, find files by name, diff files, diagnostics), terminal commands (run commands, spawn background processes), git operations, web search, and utility functions (token counting, clipboard, asking the user questions).

Use the provided tools to complete tasks. Chain multiple tool calls as needed — don't stop after a single tool call if more work is required.

CRITICAL RULES:
- After using tools, provide a final response. If the user explicitly requested exact final wording or a strict output format, follow that format exactly; otherwise provide a substantive response explaining what you found or did. NEVER stop after just executing tools.
- Don't narrate actions before doing them. Just make the tool call.
- Between consecutive tool calls, minimize text. Make the next tool call directly.
- ALWAYS read a file before editing it — edits require exact text match.
- For large files (>2000 lines), use offset/limit to read in chunks.
- Prefer batch edits for multiple changes to the same file.
- Use search tools to find code patterns instead of reading entire files.
- Use diagnostics after making code changes to catch errors early.
- Ask the user when you need clarification — don't guess.
- After ALL tool calls are complete, write a final response. If the user explicitly requested exact final wording or a strict output format, follow that format exactly; otherwise write a clear, helpful response summarizing your findings or explaining what you did.

function.description: Read the contents of a file with line numbers. Returns up to 2000 lines by default. Use offset/limit for large files.
function.name: read_file

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return. Default: 2000

offset.description: Start reading from this line number (1-based). Default: 1

path.description: File path relative to working directory

function.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file and parent directories if they don't exist, overwrites if they do.
function.name: write_file

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: File path relative to working directory

function.description: Edit a file by finding and replacing text. The search_text must match exactly (including indentation). Use read_file first to see the current content. Set replace_all=true to replace ALL occurrences (useful for renaming variables/functions).
function.name: edit_file

path.description: File path relative to working directory

replace_all.description: Replace ALL occurrences instead of just the first. Default: false

replacement_text.description: Text to replace the search_text with

search_text.description: Exact text to find in the file (must be unique unless replace_all=true)

function.description: List files and directories at a path. Shows file types and sizes.
function.name: list_directory

path.description: Directory path relative to working directory. Use "." for the root.

recursive.description: List recursively (default: false). Use sparingly on large trees.

function.description: Search file contents for a pattern using ripgrep. Returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers.
function.name: search_files

path.description: Directory to search in, relative to working directory. Default: "."

function.description: Find files by name pattern. Returns matching file paths. Useful for finding files when you don't know their exact location.
function.name: find_files

path.description: Directory to search in, relative to working directory. Default: "."

function.description: Get metadata about a file or directory: size, type (file/directory/symlink), last modified time, permissions.
function.name: file_info

function.description: Create a directory and any necessary parent directories.
function.name: create_directory

path.description: Directory path relative to working directory

function.description: Delete a file or empty directory. Use with caution — this cannot be undone.
function.name: delete_file

function.description: Move or rename a file or directory.
function.name: move_file

destination.description: Destination path relative to working directory

source.description: Source path relative to working directory

function.description: Copy a file to a new location.
function.name: copy_file

destination.description: Destination file path relative to working directory

source.description: Source file path relative to working directory

function.description: Execute a shell command in the working directory. Has a 60 second timeout. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.
function.name: run_command

function.description: Search the web using Brave Search. Returns titles, URLs, and descriptions of matching pages.
function.name: web_search

function.description: Fetch a URL and return its content as text. HTML is automatically converted to readable text. Useful for reading documentation, API responses, or web pages.
function.name: fetch_url

function.description: Search the web using DuckDuckGo (free, no API key required). Returns titles, URLs, and snippets.
function.name: ddg_search

function.description: Apply a unified diff patch to a file. Useful for making multiple related edits at once. The patch should be in standard unified diff format (--- a/file, +++ b/file, @@ hunks).
function.name: patch_file

patch.description: Unified diff patch content (with @@ hunk headers, - for removed lines, + for added lines)

path.description: File path relative to working directory

function.description: Apply multiple find-and-replace edits to a file in a single call. More efficient than multiple edit_file calls. Each edit is applied sequentially.
function.name: batch_edit

edits.description: Array of {search_text, replacement_text} edits to apply in order

path.description: File path relative to working directory

function.description: Run diagnostics on a file or project: type checking (TypeScript), linting, or syntax validation. Returns errors and warnings with file locations.
function.name: get_diagnostics

path.description: File or directory path relative to working directory. Use "." for project root.

tool.description: Diagnostic tool to run: "auto" (detect from project), "tsc" (TypeScript), "eslint", "python" (py_compile). Default: "auto"

function.description: Insert text at a specific line number in a file. The new text is inserted BEFORE the specified line. Use read_file first to see line numbers.
function.name: insert_text

line.description: Line number to insert before (1-based). Use 0 to append at end of file.

path.description: File path relative to working directory

text.description: Text to insert (can be multiple lines)

function.description: Replace a range of lines in a file with new content. Use read_file first to see line numbers.
function.name: replace_lines

path.description: File path relative to working directory

text.description: Replacement text (can be more or fewer lines than the replaced range)

function.description: Get a project directory tree respecting .gitignore rules. Shows the hierarchical file and directory structure.
function.name: get_tree

path.description: Directory path relative to working directory. Default: "."

function.description: Apply a regex find-and-replace across one or more files. Returns the number of replacements made per file.
function.name: apply_regex

glob.description: File glob filter when path is a directory (e.g., "*.ts", "*.py")

path.description: File or directory path relative to working directory

pattern.description: JavaScript regex pattern to search for

function.description: Read an image file and return its base64-encoded data with MIME type. Supports png, jpg, gif, webp, svg. Max 10MB.
function.name: read_image

path.description: Image file path relative to working directory

function.description: Read a local video file and attach it for VL model analysis. Supports mp4, mov, m4v, webm, mkv. Max 50MB.
function.name: read_video

path.description: Video file path relative to working directory

function.description: Start a long-running background process (e.g., dev server, watcher). Returns a process ID for checking output later. Auto-kills after 5 minutes.
function.name: spawn_process

function.description: Read stdout/stderr from a previously spawned background process. Returns latest output and whether the process is still running.
function.name: get_process_output

function.description: Ask the user a question and wait for their response. Use when you need clarification, confirmation, or user input to proceed with a task.
function.name: ask_user

question.description: The question to ask the user

function.description: Show a unified diff between two files, or between a file and its git HEAD version (if path_b is omitted).
function.name: diff_files

path_a.description: First file path (or the only path when comparing against git HEAD)

path_b.description: Second file path. Omit to diff against git HEAD.

function.description: Estimate the token count of a text string using character and word heuristics.
function.name: count_tokens

text.description: Text to estimate token count for

function.description: Read the current contents of the system clipboard.
function.name: clipboard_read

function.description: Write text to the system clipboard.
function.name: clipboard_write

text.description: Text to write to the clipboard

function.description: Run git commands in the working directory. Supports status, diff, log, blame, add, commit, branch, checkout, stash, show, and more. Blocks destructive operations (push --force, reset --hard).
function.name: git

command.description: Git subcommand and arguments (e.g., "status", "diff src/", "log --oneline -10", "commit -m \"fix: typo\"", "blame file.ts")

function.description: Get the current date, time, and timezone. Use this when you need to know the current date or time.
function.name: get_current_datetime
